Contextualized embeddings

•

In standard word embeddings, each word is assigned a single 
word vector, independently of its context. 

• Such a model cannot account for polysemy, the phenomenon 
that one and the same word may have multiple meanings. 

e children play in the park. e play premiered yesterday. 

•

In contextualized embeddings, each token is assigned a 
representation that depends on its context.

ELMo – Embeddings from Language Models

• A token is represented as a task-speciﬁc, weighted sum of 

representations derived from a bidirectional language model. 

weights are learned for a speciﬁc task 

• e basic ELMo model is frozen aer pre-training and can 
complement or replace a standard word embedding layer. 

• However, it is oen beneﬁcial to ﬁne-tune a pre-trained ELMo 

model on task-speciﬁc data.
